_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Extradural tumors causing spinal cord compression by epidural growth, extrinsic spinal cord compression, cauda equina compression, or intradural invasion.
Symptoms including pain (nonspecific, local, progressive, increasing at night), radicular pain, paraesthesia, limb paresis, spinal deformity, bowel/bladder disturbance.
In children, red flag symptoms like limp, fever, bruising.
Signs including spinal tenderness, spine deformity - sensory disturbance, limb weakness, or other myelopathy features.
Indication for spinal fixation, often due to instability caused by the tumor or surgical intervention (e.g., Hemangioma in case of instability).

preauth.label: Positron emission tomography (PET) scan (Based on Etiology)
CT/MRI of other regions if symptomatic (Based on Etiology)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of treatment?
Did history, physical examination, and radiological investigations confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details provided?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the imaging indicative of surgery?
Was post-op CT Spine report submitted?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge submitted?

common_queries: Clinical presentation, grading, or radiological investigations not indicative of surgery.
Indication for implant not clearly documented at pre-authorization.
Missing implant details (barcode/invoice) at claim stage.
Missing/incomplete radiological investigations (CT/MRI, PET, X-ray, Bone scan) for etiology at pre-authorization.
Histopathological/Biopsy report not submitted at claim stage.

clinical_key_pointers: Extradural tumors are usually metastatic and most often arise in the vertebral bodies.
Metastatic lesions can cause spinal cord compression either by epidural growth that results in extrinsic spinal cord or cauda equina compression or, less frequently, by intradural invasion.
The most common tumors arising in the extradural space are metastases.
Several uncommon primary tumors can also arise in the extradural space.
surgical_options: Hemangioma: Surgical resection and stabilization (if unstable)
Osteoid Osteoma: Surgery (Burr down excision or marginal resection) for pain relief, NSAID contraindication, or progressive deformity
Giant cell tumor: Enbloc resection +/- Embolization
Malignant Primary Spine Tumours: Wide or marginal excision, surgical excision, en bloc excision (depending on tumor type) often combined with radiotherapy/chemotherapy/proton beam therapy
additional_information.pre_op_preparation: Surgical fitness work-up including CBC, LFT, RFT, BT, CT, PT, INR, Serum Electrolytes, HIV, HBsAG, HCV etc.; Radiological investigation including X-ray, CT scan for bone details, MRI scan with contrast, Bone scan; Metastasis workup including Chest X-ray, Mammography, Ultrasound of abdomen, Blood tests -PSA, CT/MRI of other regions if symptomatic; Biopsy (for initial assessment or if surgery is not anticipated), CT/C-arm guided biopsy.
additional_information.fitness_criteria: Surgical fitness work-up required for assessment.
additional_information.referral_criteria: Clinicians are guided to decide referral of cases to the appropriate level of care (as required) for treatment of patients under PMJAY.
